name: Turf.js

auth.notes: Pure client/server JavaScript library; no auth required

performance.notes: In-process computation; sub-millisecond for most operations on small geometries

agent_readiness.notes: Modular JavaScript/TypeScript geospatial analysis library. 150+ functions: measurement (area, length, distance, bearing), transformation (buffer, union, intersect, simplify), classification (clusters, voronoi), interpolation (isolines, tin), coordinate mutation, random data generation. Works in browser and Node.js. GeoJSON-native. Tree-shakeable packages (@turf/area etc.). Most popular JS geospatial library.

gotchas: Operations assume WGS84 (degrees) — planar math causes errors for large areas
Tree-shaking recommended; full bundle is large
Some functions require turf-helpers-compatible GeoJSON format
best_when: Frontend map applications needing client-side spatial operations
Buffering, clipping, union of GeoJSON features
Distance/bearing calculations between coordinates in JS/TS apps
summary: Turf.js is the most popular JavaScript geospatial analysis library with 150+ modular functions. GeoJSON-native, tree-shakeable. Runs in browser and Node.js. Covers measurement (area, distance, bearing), transformation (buffer, union, intersect), clustering (voronoi), and interpolation. TypeScript support. Best for frontend map applications and Node.js spatial processing pipelines.
